origin.capability_match: Trigger on-chain transactions based on predefined logic (e.g., wrap tokens, start/end streams)

attack_surface.data_description: Auto-Wrap service initiating excessive or continuous wrapping due to a bug or misconfiguration.

security_gap.gap_description: A faulty Auto-Wrap service might deplete user's underlying tokens, denying them access to those funds in their original form.

actor_risk.description: A misbehaving Auto-Wrap service can unintentionally cause financial loss or inconvenience.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Continuous unintended wrapping can lock up user's ERC20 tokens into Super Tokens against their immediate wishes, or deplete them if wrapping to an attacker-controlled address (covered by EoP).

assumptions: The Auto-Wrap service contains a bug or is misconfigured leading to excessive wrapping.
The service has sufficient ERC20 approval to perform these excessive wraps.
Users may not immediately notice the excessive wrapping activity.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust testing and monitoring for the Auto-Wrap service. Include circuit breakers or rate limits for automated actions. Provide users with clear notifications about auto-wrap activity and easy ways to disable the service or adjust settings.

description: A faulty or misconfigured Auto-Wrap service (Actor 8) excessively wraps a user's Underlying ERC20 Tokens (Asset 102) into Super Tokens via Component 202, effectively denying the user access to their funds in the original ERC20 form and potentially incurring unwanted transaction fees.

trace_source.matched_capability: Trigger on-chain transactions based on predefined logic (e.g., wrap tokens, start/end streams)
